title: Saleor
description: GraphQL-first, headless open-source e-commerce platform for building scalable storefronts, marketplaces and digital commerce.
installation: Saleor is a multi-container stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY GraphQL API/server image (ghcr.io/saleor/saleor). After first boot, exec into the pod and run migrations + create an admin: `python manage.py migrate` then `python manage.py createsuperuser` (or set the pre-baked superuser via env). You MUST also deploy these companion services, all using the SAME ghcr.io/saleor/saleor image with the same DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL/SECRET_KEY env: (1) Celery WORKER -> command `celery --app saleor.celeryconf:app worker --loglevel=info`; (2) Celery BEAT SCHEDULER -> command `celery --app saleor.celeryconf:app beat --scheduler saleor.schedulers.schedulers.DatabaseScheduler`. The admin DASHBOARD is a separate static SPA image `ghcr.io/saleor/saleor-dashboard` (nginx, port 80) pointed at this API's /graphql/ endpoint. Add-ons: a Postgres add-on (Saleor recommends PostgreSQL) and a Valkey/Redis add-on (used as Celery broker + cache) are provisioned. Rotate the default Postgres password and the SECRET_KEY (replace the placeholder with `openssl rand -hex 32`). Set ALLOWED_HOSTS/ALLOWED_GRAPHQL_ORIGINS to your real host. For media/static uploads Saleor recommends external S3-compatible object storage in production; this template mounts a local volume at /app/media for default local file storage. A full-text search backend is optional and not included.
